name: ParticleEmitter3D

changelog.breaking: - the object rotates the other way around X axis.
- Object properties for position and rotation have been removed. They must be set with the instance editor or the action.

shortDescription: Display a large number of particles in 3D to create visual effects in a 3D game.

eventsBasedObjects.description: Display a large number of particles to create visual effects.
eventsBasedObjects.name: ParticleEmitter3D

eventsFunctions.name: HasEnded
eventsFunctions.description: Check that emission has ended and no particle is alive anymore.

eventsFunctions.name: Restart
eventsFunctions.description: Restart particule emission from the beginning.

eventsFunctions.name: StartColor
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the start color of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetStartColor
eventsFunctions.description: Change the start color of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: EndColor
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the end color of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetEndColor
eventsFunctions.description: Change the end color of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: StartOpacity

eventsFunctions.name: SetStartOpacity

eventsFunctions.name: Flow

eventsFunctions.description: Compare the start max size of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetStartSizeMax

eventsFunctions.name: EndScale

eventsFunctions.name: SetStartSpeedMin
eventsFunctions.description: Change the min start speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: StartSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the max start speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetStartSpeedMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the max start speed of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: LifespanMin
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the min lifespan of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetLifespanMin

eventsFunctions.name: LifespanMax
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the max lifespan of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetLifespanMax
eventsFunctions.description: Change the max lifespan of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: Duration
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the emission duration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: SetDuration
eventsFunctions.description: Change the emission duration of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: AreParticlesRelative
eventsFunctions.description: Check if particles move with the emitter.

eventsFunctions.name: SetAreParticlesRelative
eventsFunctions.description: Change if particles move with the emitter.

eventsFunctions.name: Blending

eventsFunctions.description: Change the gravity top of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: Gravity
eventsFunctions.description: Compare the gravity of the object.

eventsFunctions.name: ShouldAutodestruct

eventsFunctions.description: Change the start max trail length of the object.

eventsFunctions.description: Check if the trail should follow the object.

eventsFunctions.description: Change the tail end width ratio of the object.

description:
3D particle emitters let you create and display many small particles to simulate visual effects in your game — like fire, explosions, smoke, or dust.

The parameters of the object can be configured in multiple different ways to create a specific desired effect. GDevelop will give a set of a pre-configured 3D particle emitters that you should try first.

Use these 3D particle emitters in 3D games. For 2D games or particles that appear on a game's 2D user interface, use the 2D particle emitter object instead.
